description_long: This is a stone sculpture of a traditional Chinese guardian lion, also known as a 'Foo Dog' or 'Shishi', seated on a rectangular pedestal. The lion features a highly detailed, curly mane, expressive facial features with a wide-open mouth, and a decorative collar with a bell. Its right paw rests on a carved sphere, symbolizing protection and power, while the body is adorned with stylized fur and ornamental elements. The pedestal is intricately decorated with geometric patterns and motifs, adding to the sculpture's grandeur. The surface shows signs of weathering, giving it an aged, authentic appearance.
description: A detailed stone Chinese guardian lion sculpture with a curly mane, ornate collar, and decorative pedestal, exuding a sense of protection and tradition.
description_view_0: Front view showing the lion's expressive face, open mouth, detailed mane, and right paw resting on a carved sphere atop the ornate pedestal.
description_view_1: Left rear three-quarter view highlighting the lion's curled mane, back, and the intricate side details of the pedestal.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the lion's back, tail, and the full width of the pedestal's rear geometric pattern.
description_view_3: Right side view focusing on the lion's profile, mane, and the ornamental details along the side of the pedestal.
attribute_subject_matter: Chinese guardian lion (Foo Dog) seated on a decorated pedestal
attribute_location_context: No visible context; background is isolated, suggesting the sculpture is not currently installed in a specific environment.
